findings.evidence: `code/TauCeti/NumberTheory/EffectiveBounds/TraceForm.lean:111` has `(hx : ∀ r : ℚ, (r : K) ≠ x)`, while `code/TauCeti/FieldTheory/Trace.lean:65` already has `trace_eq_zero_of_sq_ratCast_of_not_mem_range ... (hx : x ∉ (algebraMap ℚ K).range)`.
findings.fix: Change the requested theorem's signature to take `(hx : x ∉ (algebraMap ℚ K).range)` and prove it from the existing theorem; add a pointwise wrapper only as a derived convenience if needed.
findings.issue: `NumberField.trace_eq_zero_of_sq_ratCast` uses the pointwise assumption `∀ r : ℚ, (r : K) ≠ x`, while the existing reusable theorem uses the standard Mathlib form `x ∉ (algebraMap ℚ K).range`; this makes the requested roadmap helper less composable than the nearby API.

findings.evidence: `code/TauCeti/NumberTheory/EffectiveBounds/TraceForm.lean:55` proves `trace_eq_zero_of_sq_mem_range`; `code/TauCeti/FieldTheory/Trace.lean:32` already proves the same finite-extension statement privately as `trace_eq_zero_of_sq_algebraMap_of_not_mem_range`.
findings.fix: Promote or rename the existing private `trace_eq_zero_of_sq_algebraMap_of_not_mem_range` in `TauCeti/FieldTheory/Trace.lean`, import it here, and make this file only provide the discriminant application/special aliases.
findings.issue: The new public finite-extension theorem reproves the same general trace lemma that already exists privately in `TauCeti/FieldTheory/Trace.lean`, leaving the general field API split between an EffectiveBounds file and the trace collection file.

summary: The trace criterion is present, but the public number-field API is less general than the existing standard range-based form and duplicates nearby trace material instead of exposing it at the reusable level.
